title: Starmer démissionne, Burnham arrive
gist: Keir Starmer stepped down as prime minister after Nigel Farage's Reform party overtook Labour and voters turned against him. Andy Burnham, a popular, charismatic former mayor, is expected to take over — but Britain's deep problems (big debt, weak growth) remain, and his policies may not differ much. The article's point: charisma alone won't be enough.

chunks: Keir Starmer resigns — present tense for a fresh event
bd.key: démissionner = 'to resign'. The present tense is the simplest way to report news: 'Starmer resigns'.

body.fr: Il {{n'est plus|is no longer}} Premier ministre.

bd.meaning: He is no longer Prime Minister.
chunks: he is no longer (the ne...plus sandwich)
bd.key: A new sandwich filling: ne ... plus = 'no longer / not anymore' — same shape as ne...pas, different word. ne becomes n' before the vowel.

body.fr: Le parti de Nigel Farage, {{Reform|Reform (a party)}}, est très {{populaire|popular}}.

bd.meaning: Nigel Farage's party, Reform, is very popular.
chunks: Nigel Farage's party (French: 'the party OF Farage')
bd.key: Possession with 'de': French has no 's. 'Farage's party' becomes le parti de Nigel Farage, 'the party of Farage'.
c'est = 'it is / this is' — the go-to set phrase for describing or identifying something.

body.fr: {{Beaucoup de gens|many people}} {{ne veulent plus|no longer want}} de Starmer.

bd.meaning: Many people no longer want Starmer.
bd.key: ne ... plus once more: ne veulent plus = 'don't want anymore'. The sandwich wraps the verb veulent.

body.fr: Le Royaume-Uni {{a beaucoup de problèmes|has many problems}}.

chunks: many problems (beaucoup de = a lot of)
bd.key: beaucoup de = 'a lot of / many' — always with 'de', never 'des': beaucoup de problèmes.
French keeps 'the' for general ideas: la croissance = just 'growth', not 'the growth'.

chunks: is very big ('grande' takes -e because 'dette' is feminine)
bd.key: Adjective agreement: dette is feminine, so grand becomes grande.

body.fr: En dix ans, le pays {{a eu|had}} six Premiers ministres.

bd.meaning: In ten years, the country has had six prime ministers.
chunks: the country has had (a eu = avoir + the past form of avoir itself)
bd.key: Even 'to have' has a past: a eu = 'has had / had' (avoir + eu).

body.fr: Bientôt, il va {{avoir|have}} un {{septième|seventh}} Premier ministre.

bd.meaning: Soon, it's going to have a seventh prime minister.
chunks: it's going to have (aller + plain verb)
bd.key: aller + plain verb future: va avoir = 'is going to have'. septième = 'seventh' (numbers add -ième).

body.fr: Le {{nouveau chef|new leader}} va probablement {{être|be}} Andy Burnham.

bd.meaning: The new leader will probably be Andy Burnham.
chunks: the new leader ('nouveau' comes BEFORE the noun)
is probably going to be (aller + plain verb, with 'probablement' slipped in)
bd.key: 'nouveau' goes before the noun — one of the small group that do. And the future is va être = 'is going to be'.

body.fr: Il est {{ancien maire|former mayor}} de Manchester.

bd.meaning: He is the former mayor of Manchester.
bd.key: Position changes meaning: ancien maire (before) = 'former mayor'; un bâtiment ancien (after) = 'an old building'.
Two easy cognates: populaire, charismatique — both end in -e and don't change for gender.
rester = 'to stay / remain' — a false friend: it does NOT mean 'to rest'. Plural subject, so restent.

body.fr: Sa {{politique|policy}} {{ne va peut-être pas|maybe won't}} beaucoup changer.

bd.meaning: His policy may not change much.
chunks: is maybe not going to (ne...pas around 'va', with 'peut-être' = maybe inside)
bd.key: Negating the aller-future: the ne ... pas wraps va (the helper), not the plain verb: ne va pas changer = 'is not going to change'.

chunks: is not enough (ne...pas around 'suffit', from suffire = to be enough)
bd.key: The ne ... pas sandwich once more, around suffit: 'is not enough'. Learn the shape once, spot it everywhere.
devoir + plain verb = must / have to: doit obtenir = 'must get'. The helper conjugates; the second verb stays plain.

grammar.en: The negation sandwich and its cousin. ne ... pas = 'not'; ne ... plus = 'no longer'. Both wrap the verb (n'est plus, ne veulent plus, ne suffit pas).

grammar.fr: va être / va avoir / va changer
grammar.en: aller + plain verb = the near future, used all through this story to talk about what's coming. Only aller changes.
A few adjectives go BEFORE the noun (nouveau, ancien, grand, bon). And ancien even shifts meaning by position: before = 'former', after = 'old / ancient'.

quiz.q: Pourquoi le charisme ne suffit pas ?
quiz.a: Parce que les problèmes du Royaume-Uni (la dette, la croissance faible) restent. (Because the UK's problems — debt, weak growth — remain.)

flag: Also compressed hard from a long opinion piece: I dropped Reform's poll numbers, the debt figure (94% of GDP), the nicknames and the magazine jab at Starmer, and kept it neutral rather than carrying the article's editorial tone. One register note: ne veulent plus de Starmer is natural but a touch colloquial; ne soutiennent plus Starmer ('no longer support') is the more standard news phrasing.
